medications: Acetaminophen as needed for back pain
health.mental_health: Since immigrating he has intermittent anxiety about paperwork and job stability, occasional sleep disruption on long-haul weeks, and feels isolated without extended family nearby; he has not pursued formal counseling yet.
recent_visits: DOT physical exam at an occupational health clinic (renewal paperwork for commercial driving)
Urology follow-up for urinary symptoms and prostate medication adjustment
Primary care visit to establish care in the U.S. and review blood pressure readings
Pharmacy consultation about switching to lower-cost generics

finance.debt: Car payment on a used sedan and a small personal loan used for immigration/legal fees and initial housing deposits; he is also sending regular remittances to family abroad while building U.S. credit.
investments: Small U.S. checking/savings account with emergency fund
Fixed deposit account in his home country (legacy savings)
recent_transactions: Payment to an immigration attorney for document filing support
First-time purchase of U.S. health insurance premium through employer plan
Large purchase: used smartphone and prepaid plan upgrade for navigation and translation
Rent and security deposit for a room in a shared house in Raleigh

relationships: Wife, Meena Singh (still abroad; daily calls while they plan reunification paperwork)
Adult son, Arjun Singh (lives in New Jersey; helps with online forms and banking)
Cousin, Harpreet Singh (lives in Cary, NC; occasional rides and help navigating local services)
Neighbor/housemate, Luis Martinez (shared rental; occasional language and errands support)
Gurdwara community in Morrisville (new social circle; attends when not on the road)
social.social_media_activity: Uses WhatsApp and Facebook mostly to keep in touch with family abroad and a few local community groups; keeps posts minimal, avoids sharing his address, and rarely tags locations.
private_conversations: Discussions with his wife about immigration timelines, financial strain, and loneliness
Conversations with his son about credit building, bank account access, and avoiding scams
Confiding in a cousin about urinary symptoms and worries about passing DOT medical requirements
Questions in community chats about trustworthy clinics, affordable prescriptions, and safe neighborhoods

schedule.work_hours: Typically 50–60 hours/week; early starts (3–5 a.m.) with 1–3 overnight trips most weeks depending on dispatch and routes across NC/VA/SC.
regular_appointments: Blood pressure check at home 3–4 mornings per week (logged for primary care)
Monthly pharmacy refill pickup (often coordinated around return-to-Raleigh days)
Quarterly primary care follow-up for hypertension management
Gurdwara service when in town (usually Sunday morning)
upcoming_events: DOT medical card renewal paperwork submission window (within the next month)
Video call with immigration attorney to review family sponsorship documents
DMV appointment for license/address update and Real ID documentation check
Weekend visit to cousin in Cary to review mail and bills together
schedule.daily_routine: On workdays he checks blood pressure, packs a cooler, reviews route and delivery windows, drives long stretches with scheduled breaks, then does a brief walk and calls family before sleep; on home days he catches up on mail, appointments, and community visits.

professional.company: Regional freight carrier based in the Triangle area (dry van and some refrigerated loads) contracted with big-box retailers and distribution centers
projects: Completing company onboarding and safety modules (English-language training)
Learning electronic logging device (ELD) workflows and U.S. DOT compliance rules
Route familiarization for Raleigh-to-Richmond and Raleigh-to-Charlotte lanes
Improving fuel efficiency metrics to qualify for monthly bonus
professional.performance_issues: He is careful and punctual but sometimes needs extra time with dispatch calls and paperwork due to accent/language confidence; he worries that urinary urgency and sleep disruption could affect long-haul comfort and DOT compliance, so he plans routes around reliable rest stops.
workplace_relationships: Supportive dispatcher who prefers texting instructions to reduce call misunderstandings
A senior driver mentor who helped him learn local distribution center procedures
Tension with one dock supervisor after a documentation mix-up during his first month

hobbies: Walking in nearby parks (Dorothea Dix Park when in town)
Listening to Punjabi and old Hindi music while driving
Cooking simple vegetarian meals on days off
Watching cricket highlights and local news
Volunteering occasionally at the gurdwara kitchen (seva) when schedule allows
habits: Drinks strong chai most mornings and tries to limit it on long-haul days due to urinary frequency
Keeps a paper folder of immigration, medical, and employment documents and checks it repeatedly to avoid losing paperwork
Uses translation apps for mail, clinic forms, and employer portals
Carries a urine bottle in the cab for emergencies (embarrassing but practical on long stretches)
lifestyle.living_situation: Rents a furnished room in a shared house in southeast Raleigh while he saves money and works on bringing his wife to the U.S.; keeps a low profile and is cautious about sharing his address.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Mostly vegetarian with low-salt focus for blood pressure; tries to choose grilled options on the road, limits fried foods, and packs fruit, yogurt, and nuts to avoid truck-stop meals.
